_doc: First AI-assisted human review pass on CILogBench v1.3.
Single reviewer (project author), worked from a ChatGPT draft and
verified each of the 48 items. NOT independent human review.
Verdict per E9 plan rules: WEAKEN_HEADLINE — human pairwise leans
grep (8 wins vs 2 hybrid out of 16, with 6 ties), but hybrid stays
inside the 0.25 absolute-usefulness tie band and keeps the ~3x cost

reviewers._note: Kept in labels dir for audit. Not a second independent reviewer; reviewer_human_a's labels are byte-identical because they verified each item and chose not to override.

headline_numbers._spearman_caveat: Score-compression artifact: 29/32 absolute scores are 4. Spearman dominated by tie-breaking noise.
headline_numbers._confErr_finding: sv1.1 had 0 false positives on the 32 reviewed items. Reproduces E2b finding for the third time.

verdict_basis: human pairwise prefers grep 8-to-2 (out of 10 decisive)
absolute mean overall_usefulness within 0.25 tie band (Δ=0.063)
macro total pipeline tokens unchanged: hybrid ~5k vs grep ~16k (~3x)
Spearman with sv1.1 fails 0.5 threshold but is dominated by score compression
v1_3_doc_changes_required: docs/reports/cilogbench_v1_3_limitations.md       ← add §2 Update (2026-05) on AI-assisted human review
next_steps: Optional: second independent human reviewer for real IRR
Optional: larger corpus to widen score variance
Do NOT modify sv1.1 based solely on this pass (score compression caveat)
